progressionRules.rule: Hold the weight until all three sets hit 12 clean reps, then add 2.5 kg.

exerciseLibrary.name: Goblet squat to box
exerciseLibrary.coachNext: Hold 20 kg and build to 3×15. The tibia sets the pace, not your ego.

exerciseLibrary.name: Dumbbell bench press
exerciseLibrary.coachNext: Stay at 22 kg until all three sets hit 12 clean — then 24 kg.

exerciseLibrary.name: Cable stack lat pulldown
exerciseLibrary.coachNext: 55 kg next session. The 50s moved well.

exerciseLibrary.name: Plate-loaded lat pulldown
exerciseLibrary.coachNext: Earn 50 kg × 3×12 first — the last set hit 10 today.

exerciseLibrary.name: Cable lateral raise
exerciseLibrary.coachNext: Stay at 5 kg, build from 3×10 to 3×12. Delts grow on reps, not load.

exerciseLibrary.name: Seated cable row (close grip)
exerciseLibrary.coachNext: Retest — that number looks generous next to your wide-grip row.

exerciseLibrary.name: Tricep pushdown
exerciseLibrary.coachNext: Set a baseline this week: pick a weight you can move for 3×12.

coachNotes.title: Fasted session — PBs and one telling-off
coachNotes.note: Bench 22 kg for 12, 10, 13 — set two wobbled, set three answered. Hold 22 until 3x12 is clean. You trained fasted: 40–50 g protein within the hour. No exceptions.
lines: Goblet squat to box: 20 kg for 3×12 — that is +60% on baseline in ten days. We hold here and build to 3×15 before adding load. The tibia sets the pace, not your ego.
Bench: 22 kg for 12, 10, 13. Set two wobbled, set three answered. Keep 22 kg until all three sets hit 12 clean.
Plate pulldown: 50 kg × 12, × 12, then × 10 — the last set told the truth. Stay at 50.
You trained fasted. Fine — but 40–50 g protein within the hour. No exceptions.
Now the uncomfortable bit: six days between sessions #2 and #3. Life got busy — I know. Your rule is never more than three. Book the next one before you leave the building.

coachNotes.title: Good density, better decisions
coachNotes.note: Row moved to 40 kg mid-session — right call. Pulldown ladder 40/45/50: 50 kg is the working weight now. 49 minutes at avg HR 132 is good density.
lines: Row: 35 kg moved too fast, so you went to 40 mid-session. Right call — that is what auto-regulation looks like.
Pulldown ladder 40/45/50: 50 kg is your working weight now.
49 minutes, average HR 132. Good density for a strength session — you are not resting on your phone.
Protein was excellent this week. Keep the streak.

coachNotes.title: Baseline day
coachNotes.note: Baselines set across the board. Squat starts light on purpose — technique before bravado. Weigh in every morning: the scale is data, not judgement.
lines: We started the squat light at 12.5 kg on purpose. You are cleared to train, not cleared to be daft — lower body progresses on technique, not bravado.
Bench at 18 kg and seated row at 60 kg give us clean starting points. Everything here has room to climb quickly.
Weigh in every morning. The scale is data, not judgement.
